articles.title: APPLE PLANNING ‘BOLD' NEW 20TH ANNIVERSARY DESIGN FOR IPHONE 19 PRO
articles.summary: Apple is reportedly planning a major iPhone redesign for its 20th anniversary next year that will include a foldable model and a bold new Pro version inspired by Jony Ive's vision of a seamless glass design. Speculation suggests Apple may name the standard iPhone 18 and the anniversary edition iPhone 20, similar to the iPhone X naming strategy.

articles.title: CHATGPT USERS HAVE GENERATED OVER 700M IMAGES SINCE LAST WEEK, OPENAI SAYS
articles.summary: OpenAI's upgraded image generator in ChatGPT has drawn over 130 million users and 700 million images since March 25, causing capacity issues and product delays. Its viral Ghibli-style feature fueled growth, especially in India, but also strained infrastructure. OpenAI is working nonstop to meet the overwhelming demand.

articles.title: GOOGLE'S AI MODE CAN NOW SEE AND SEARCH WITH IMAGES
articles.summary: Google has expanded AI Mode to millions more users in the US, adding the ability to analyze and respond to image-based queries. Powered by Gemini AI and Google Lens, it provides detailed answers with links and recommendations from photos. The update enhances visual search and positions AI Mode as a rival to ChatGPT Search and Perplexity.

articles.title: WHAT IT'S LIKE TO BE A DESIGNER AT 40
articles.summary: A 40-year-old UX designer reflects on the current state of the industry, expressing concerns about its creative decline and the overwhelming noise online. While acknowledging AI's potential to simplify workflows, he emphasizes the enduring value of human creativity and the need to stay curious and adaptable.

articles.title: CURSOR, “VIBE CODING,” AND MANUS: THE UX REVOLUTION THAT AI NEEDS
articles.summary: Current AI interfaces often rely on rigid "prompt engineering," requiring users to input specific phrases and formats to get accurate results — a limitation rooted in design, not intelligence. To make AI more accessible, there's a growing push for more intuitive interactions, including tools like Cursor and casual, conversational approaches like "vibe coding."

articles.title: MAKE DESIGN FUN AGAIN
articles.summary: Design often begins with playful curiosity—like building with Legos or sketching for fun—before becoming a career. However, professional demands can drain that joy. Designers need to reconnect with playful creation, free from goals or judgment, and rediscover the pleasure of making purely for themselves, not for work.

articles.title: FREE PREMIUM MOCKUPS
articles.summary: A collection of more than 23,000 free mockups that include T-shirts, devices, mugs, hats, books, bags, and more.

articles.title: AUTOMATE MANUAL RESEARCH
articles.summary: Ottogrid uses AI agents to research, enrich data, and process documents in minutes. It also has built-in tools for scraping, web research, and lead enrichment.

articles.title: 3D DESIGN IN YOUR BROWSER
articles.summary: Create stunning 3D designs with professional tools in your browser. Womp offers revolutionary real-time goopy 3D for mind-blowing renders, animations, and 3D prints.

articles.title: POW! EIGA'S ENERGETIC BRAND FOR HANOVER PUTS THE CITY'S CULTURE AND COMMUNITY SPIRIT IN THE SPOTLIGHT
articles.summary: EIGA created a branding campaign for Hanover, Germany, centered on the value proposition "Feel the Energy" to reflect the city's culture and community spirit. The "POW" logo, which features six circles and a distinctive red hue linking to the city's coat of arms, symbolizes unity among cultural institutions.

articles.title: THE GENERATIVE AI PROTOTYPE REVOLUTION: MY WEEKEND WITH LOVABLE.DEV AND TULIPS
articles.summary: Generative AI lets designers prototype in hours, not days, automating repetitive UI tasks and generating specs. It's a paradigm shift that blends human insight with machine speed. While not production-ready, it's ideal for ideation and early-stage design. Adaptation is key—AI augments workflows, redefines collaboration, and elevates the designer's role to the strategic architect.

articles.title: ON CREATIVITY AS A PROCESS OF LAYERING
articles.summary: Creativity is a collaborative process shaped by dialogue, memory, and place. Multidisciplinary work blends music and writing into evolving communal experiences. We must challenge dominant narratives, embrace ambiguity, and value repetition as a resonance form. Creativity emerges through curiosity, empathy, and lived experience.

articles.title: CROWN CREATIVE CRAFTS A RELAXED YET REFINED IDENTITY FOR A BOUTIQUE HOTEL IN OREGON'S WINE COUNTRY
articles.summary: Crown Creative's branding for Piquette, a boutique hotel in Oregon wine country, blends playful typography, tactile colors, and repurposed materials to reflect its soulful, irreverent spirit.

articles.title: DEMYSTIFYING VIDEO CODECS: A GRAPHIC DESIGNER'S GUIDE TO COMPRESSION
articles.summary: Understanding codecs and video formats helps designers manage file sizes, maintain quality, and ensure platform compatibility, improving workflow and enabling high-impact, professional video content creation.

articles.title: THE NINTENDO SWITCH 2 DREW DESIGN INSPIRATION FROM OUR COLLECTIVE NIGHTMARE
articles.summary: The Switch 2 will introduce social-focused features like GameChat and camera-sharing inspired by pandemic-era collaboration, along with Joy-Cons doubling as mice for enhanced gameplay versatility.
